acceptanceCriteria: AcpAgentAdapter implements the full AgentAdapter interface (run, complete, plan, decompose, isInstalled, buildCommand)
agent.run() uses ACP persistent session (start → createSession → prompt → close) instead of Bun.spawn
adapter.complete() uses ACP one-shot mode and returns text response
AgentResult.estimatedCost is calculated from ACP cumulative_token_usage, not regex parsing
Acceptance generators use adapter.complete() instead of raw Bun.spawn
AcpInteractionBridge forwards sessionUpdate notifications to nax interaction chain
Human responses from interaction chain are sent as follow-up prompts to the same ACP session
Config toggle: protocol='acp' uses new adapter, protocol='cli' falls back to existing adapters
Timeout handling uses ACP cooperative cancel instead of SIGTERM/SIGKILL
All existing tests pass — zero regressions

userStories.title: Fix acceptance generators to use adapter.complete()
userStories.description:
Refactor `src/acceptance/generator.ts` and `src/acceptance/fix-generator.ts` to use `adapter.complete()` instead of directly spawning `Bun.spawn([adapter.binary, ...])`. This is tech debt that must be fixed before ACP migration.

Changes:

1. In `generator.ts`, replace the `Bun.spawn([adapter.binary, "--model", ...])` block (~30 lines around line 301) with a call to `adapter.complete(prompt, { model: options.modelDef.model })`. The adapter is already available in scope.

2. In `fix-generator.ts`, replace the equivalent `Bun.spawn` block (~30 lines around line 231) with `adapter.complete(prompt, { model: modelDef.model })`.

3. Remove the direct dependency on `adapter.binary` in both files — the adapter interface should be the only contract.

4. Update the injectable `_deps` objects to use `adapter` instead of raw spawn, matching the pattern in `classifier.ts` and `refinement.ts`.

Write tests verifying the adapter.complete() calls in `test/unit/acceptance/generator-adapter.test.ts`.
acceptanceCriteria: generator.ts calls adapter.complete() instead of Bun.spawn for LLM calls
fix-generator.ts calls adapter.complete() instead of Bun.spawn for LLM calls
Neither file references adapter.binary directly
Injectable _deps pattern used for testability
Existing acceptance generator tests still pass
New test file verifies adapter.complete() is called with correct prompt and model

routing.reasoning: Straightforward refactor following established _deps pattern seen in classifier.ts and refinement.ts. No new architecture — just replacing Bun.spawn with adapter.complete().

userStories.title: ACP adapter core — run() and complete() via AcpClient
userStories.description:
Create the core `AcpAgentAdapter` class implementing `AgentAdapter` interface, using acpx's `AcpClient` for agent communication.

New files:

1. `src/agents/acp/adapter.ts` — `AcpAgentAdapter` class:
   - Constructor takes `agentName: string` (e.g., 'claude', 'codex', 'gemini')
   - `resolveAgentCommand(agentName)` maps name to ACP command via acpx agent registry
   - `run(options)` → AcpClient.start() → createSession() → prompt() → close() → return AgentResult
   - `complete(prompt, options?)` → same flow but one-shot, extract text from response
   - `isInstalled()` → check if agent binary exists on PATH
   - `buildCommand(options)` → return the ACP command array (for dry-run display)
   - Map `AgentResult` fields from ACP response: success from stopReason, cost from cumulative_token_usage, output from session messages
   - Rate limit retry: detect rate limit from ACP error, retry with backoff (same logic as current ClaudeCodeAdapter)
   - Timeout: use cooperative cancel via `client.cancelActivePrompt()` with fallback to close()

2. `src/agents/acp/types.ts` — ACP-specific types:
   - `AcpAdapterConfig` — agent name, permission mode, timeouts
   - `AcpRunContext` — internal state during a run

3. `src/agents/acp/index.ts` — barrel exports

Dependency: `acpx` (add to package.json). Import `AcpClient` and types from acpx.

Permission mode: always `approve-all` for headless execution (matches current `--dangerously-skip-permissions`).

Write tests in `test/unit/agents/acp/adapter.test.ts`. Mock `AcpClient` — do not spawn real agents.
acceptanceCriteria: AcpAgentAdapter implements AgentAdapter interface with all required methods
run() creates AcpClient, starts session, sends prompt, returns AgentResult
complete() works in one-shot mode and returns trimmed text response
AgentResult.estimatedCost uses cumulative_token_usage from ACP session
AgentResult.success maps correctly from ACP stopReason (end_turn=true, cancelled/error=false)
Rate limit retry works with exponential backoff (3 attempts max)
Timeout triggers cooperative cancel via AcpClient
isInstalled() checks if agent binary exists on PATH
All AcpClient interactions are mockable via injectable _deps pattern

routing.reasoning: Core architectural change — new adapter wrapping external AcpClient library, mapping between two different result/error models, timeout handling, retry logic. Requires careful interface mapping and thorough mocking.

userStories.title: Registry integration and config toggle
userStories.description:
Integrate `AcpAgentAdapter` into nax's agent registry with a config toggle to switch between ACP and legacy CLI adapters.

Changes:

1. `src/agents/registry.ts` — update `getAgent(name)` to return `AcpAgentAdapter` when config specifies `protocol: 'acp'`, or legacy adapter when `protocol: 'cli'` (default: 'cli' for backward compatibility).

2. `src/config/schema.ts` — add `agent.protocol` field to NaxConfig: `'acp' | 'cli'` (default: 'cli'). Add `agent.acpPermissionMode` (default: 'approve-all').

3. Registry should create ONE `AcpAgentAdapter` per agent name (not per call) — reuse across the run.

4. Update `checkAgentHealth()` and `getInstalledAgents()` to work with ACP adapter.

5. Update `src/execution/lifecycle/run-initialization.ts` to log which protocol is active.

Write tests in `test/unit/agents/acp/registry.test.ts`.
acceptanceCriteria: getAgent('claude') returns AcpAgentAdapter when config.agent.protocol is 'acp'
getAgent('claude') returns ClaudeCodeAdapter when config.agent.protocol is 'cli' or unset
Config schema accepts agent.protocol field with 'acp' or 'cli' values
Default protocol is 'cli' for backward compatibility
AcpAgentAdapter instances are reused per agent name within a run
checkAgentHealth() works with both ACP and legacy adapters
Run initialization logs the active protocol

routing.reasoning: Config toggle + registry wiring. Follows existing patterns. Main complexity is ensuring backward compatibility and correct adapter selection logic.

userStories.title: Interaction bridge — sessionUpdate to interaction chain
userStories.description:
Create `AcpInteractionBridge` that connects ACP `sessionUpdate` notifications to nax's existing interaction chain, enabling mid-session agent ↔ human communication.

New file: `src/agents/acp/interaction-bridge.ts`

1. `AcpInteractionBridge` class:
   - Constructor takes `InteractionPlugin` (from `src/interaction/types.ts`) and bridge config
   - `onSessionUpdate(notification: SessionNotification)` — called by AcpClient's onSessionUpdate callback
   - Detects question patterns in agent messages ("?", "which", "should I", "unclear", "please clarify")
   - When question detected: creates `InteractionRequest` with type='input', sends via interaction chain
   - `waitForResponse(requestId, timeout)` — blocks until human responds via interaction chain
   - `getFollowUpPrompt(response)` — formats human response as follow-up prompt

2. Update `AcpAgentAdapter.run()` to:
   - Accept optional `InteractionPlugin` (from pipeline context `ctx.interaction`)
   - Create bridge if interaction plugin is available
   - When bridge receives a question: pause prompt, send to interaction chain, wait for response, send follow-up prompt to same session

3. Bridge respects timeout — if human doesn't respond within configured time, falls back to 'continue' (agent decides).

4. Bridge emits structured events for logging/reporting.

Write tests in `test/unit/agents/acp/interaction-bridge.test.ts`. Mock both AcpClient and InteractionPlugin.
acceptanceCriteria: AcpInteractionBridge detects question patterns in sessionUpdate notifications
Detected questions are forwarded as InteractionRequests to the interaction chain
Human responses are formatted and sent as follow-up prompts to the same ACP session
Timeout fallback: if no response within configured time, agent continues autonomously
Bridge integrates with existing interaction plugins (Telegram, CLI, webhook)
Non-question sessionUpdate messages are ignored by the bridge (no false positives)
Bridge lifecycle is tied to the ACP session — created on run(), destroyed on close()

routing.reasoning: Novel bridge pattern connecting two async systems (ACP notifications and interaction chain). Requires careful timeout handling, question detection heuristics, and session state management during mid-prompt pauses.

userStories.title: Plan and decompose via ACP
userStories.description:
Implement `plan()` and `decompose()` methods on `AcpAgentAdapter` using ACP sessions.

Changes to `src/agents/acp/adapter.ts`:

1. `plan(options: PlanOptions)` — for non-interactive: use one-shot ACP session, send planning prompt, return spec content from response. For interactive: use persistent session with terminal I/O bridging (defer to future — return error for now).

2. `decompose(options: DecomposeOptions)` — one-shot ACP session. Send decompose prompt, parse JSON response into DecomposedStory[]. Reuse existing `buildDecomposePrompt()` and `parseDecomposeOutput()` from `claude-decompose.ts`.

3. Both methods support model override via `options.modelDef`.

Write tests in `test/unit/agents/acp/plan-decompose.test.ts`.
acceptanceCriteria: plan() returns PlanResult with specContent from ACP session response
plan() in non-interactive mode works end-to-end via ACP one-shot
plan() in interactive mode throws clear 'not yet supported via ACP' error
decompose() returns DecomposeResult with parsed stories from ACP response
decompose() reuses existing buildDecomposePrompt and parseDecomposeOutput functions
Both methods respect model override from options.modelDef
Both methods handle ACP errors gracefully with clear error messages

routing.reasoning: Adapting existing plan/decompose logic to use ACP instead of Bun.spawn. Most complexity is already handled by the existing prompt builders and parsers — this is mainly wiring.

userStories.title: ACP cost tracking from token usage
userStories.description:
Replace regex-based cost estimation with ACP's `cumulative_token_usage` data.

Changes:

1. `src/agents/acp/cost.ts` — new file:
   - `estimateCostFromTokenUsage(usage: SessionTokenUsage, model: string): number` — calculate USD cost from token counts using per-model pricing table
   - Pricing table for common models: claude-sonnet-4, claude-haiku, gpt-4.1, codex, gemini-2.5-pro, etc.
   - Unknown model fallback: use average $/token rate
   - Handle cache tokens: `cache_read_input_tokens` at reduced rate, `cache_creation_input_tokens` at creation rate

2. Update `AcpAgentAdapter.toAgentResult()` to use the new cost calculation.

3. Export `estimateCostFromTokenUsage` for use by reporters/metrics.

Keep existing `src/agents/cost.ts` for legacy CLI adapter fallback.

Write tests in `test/unit/agents/acp/cost.test.ts`.
acceptanceCriteria: estimateCostFromTokenUsage calculates cost from input_tokens and output_tokens
Cache tokens (cache_read, cache_creation) use correct reduced/creation rates
Known models have accurate per-token pricing
Unknown models fall back to a reasonable average rate
Zero token usage returns $0.00 cost
Cost calculation is used by AcpAgentAdapter.toAgentResult()

routing.reasoning: Pure function — pricing table lookup + arithmetic. No external dependencies beyond types. Clear inputs and outputs.

userStories.title: TDD three-session flow via ACP persistent sessions
userStories.description:
Update the TDD three-session flow to use ACP persistent sessions instead of spawning 3 separate CLI processes.

Changes to `src/tdd/session-runner.ts`:

1. `runTddSession()` already receives an `AgentAdapter` and calls `agent.run()`. No changes needed here — the ACP adapter handles the protocol difference internally.

2. However, the current flow creates a fresh process per session (test-writer → implementer → verifier). With ACP, we can optionally reuse the same session for all 3 roles by sending follow-up prompts. This is an optimization, not a requirement.

3. For Phase 1: verify that the existing TDD flow works correctly with `AcpAgentAdapter.run()`. Each TDD session creates its own ACP session (3 sessions total). This is the simplest migration path.

4. Update `src/tdd/rectification-gate.ts` to work with ACP adapter — same pattern (just calls agent.run()).

Write integration tests in `test/integration/agents/acp/tdd-flow.test.ts` verifying the 3-session sequence works with mocked AcpClient.
acceptanceCriteria: TDD three-session flow works with AcpAgentAdapter (test-writer → implementer → verifier)
Each TDD session creates its own ACP session (3 total per story)
TDD rectification gate works with AcpAgentAdapter
Isolation checks still work correctly after ACP sessions
Auto-commit after each session still works
Cost tracking accumulates correctly across 3 ACP sessions

routing.reasoning: The TDD flow already goes through agent.run() — main work is integration testing to verify the ACP adapter handles the 3-session sequence correctly. No structural changes to TDD code.
